emergent_themes.description: Students who have undergone significant financial learning experiences that reshape their delivery behavior from frequent to controlled usage
Methodical comparison of platforms by building baskets on multiple apps to optimize value, going beyond simple price checking
Delivery positioned as a reward system tied to achievements, effort, or milestones rather than simple convenience
Preference for separate individual orders even when living with multiple housemates due to preference diversity and convenience

coding_summary: P030 demonstrates a sophisticated financial evolution from first-year spending freedom to second-year disciplined consumption. His delivery behavior is characterized by systematic multi-platform comparison, treating orders as earned rewards tied to achievements or effort. Despite living with 5 housemates, he predominantly orders individually due to preference diversity. His decision-making process involves methodical basket-building across Deliveroo and Uber Eats to optimize value. He shows strong awareness of subscription benefits and their role in platform choice, with clear post-graduation expectations of continued or increased usage driven by work demands.

coverage_gaps.reason: P030 shows no evidence of stigma around solo ordering - he predominantly orders alone and views it as normal behavior
